title: Worst Negative Slack Finder
description:
You are given a list of timing paths, each formatted as "path_name:slack" where slack is a float.

Write a function find_wns_path(paths) that returns the name of the path with the worst (most negative) slack.

If no path has negative slack, return "None".
constraints: 1 ≤ len(paths) ≤ 1000. Slack values are floats. Path names are alphanumeric strings.

# Test
paths = ["path_a:-0.52", "path_b:0.31", "path_c:-1.23", "path_d:-0.07"]
print(find_wns_path(paths))

title: DEF Component Instance Counter
description:
In Physical Design, a DEF file lists all placed instances.

Given a list of strings where each string is "INSTANCE_NAME CELL_TYPE", write a function count_instances(components) that returns a dictionary mapping each cell type to its instance count.

Sort the result by count in descending order.
sampleInput:
components = ["U1 AND2", "U2 OR2", "U3 AND2", "U4 INV", "U5 AND2", "U6 OR2"]
print(count_instances(components))
constraints: 1 ≤ len(components) ≤ 10000. Each string has exactly two space-separated tokens.

starterCode.python:

    pass

# Test
components = ["U1 AND2", "U2 OR2", "U3 AND2", "U4 INV", "U5 AND2", "U6 OR2"]
print(count_instances(components))

title: High Fanout Net Detection
description:
High fanout nets cause timing and signal integrity issues in Physical Design.

Given a dictionary where each key is a net name and each value is a list of sink pin names, and an integer threshold, write a function find_high_fanout_nets(nets, threshold) that returns a list of tuples (net_name, fanout) for all nets with fanout strictly greater than threshold.

Sort results by fanout in descending order.
constraints: 1 ≤ len(nets) ≤ 5000. Threshold is a positive integer.

# Test
nets = {"clk": ["FF1/CK","FF2/CK","FF3/CK","FF4/CK","FF5/CK"], "reset": ["FF1/R","FF2/R"], "data": ["FF6/D"]}
threshold = 3
print(find_high_fanout_nets(nets, threshold))

title: Clock Domain Crossing Detector
description:
Clock Domain Crossing (CDC) is a critical concern in SoC physical design.

Given a list of (source_ff, dest_ff) tuples representing data paths and a dictionary mapping each flip-flop to its clock domain, write a function detect_cdc(paths, domains) that returns all (source, destination) pairs where the two flip-flops belong to different clock domains.

Return results in the same order they appear in the input.
constraints: 1 ≤ len(paths) ≤ 10000. All flip-flops in paths are guaranteed to be present in domains.
